vulnerabilities.description: Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header is missing. CSP helps prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by specifying which content sources are allowed.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add a Content-Security-Policy header. Example: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'

vulnerabilities.description: Referrer-Policy header is missing. This header controls how much referrer information is sent with requests, protecting user privacy.
Permissions-Policy header is missing. This header controls which browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation) the page can use.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add Permissions-Policy header. Example: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

vulnerabilities.description: Server header exposes server software: 'envoy'. Attackers can use this to find known vulnerabilities.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove or obfuscate the Server header in your web server configuration.
Ensure only necessary ports are open. Review firewall rules.
Ensure only necessary ports are open. Review firewall rules.

vulnerabilities.description: Path '/crossdomain.xml' is publicly accessible (HTTP 200). This may expose sensitive data or admin interfaces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Restrict access to '/crossdomain.xml' using authentication, firewall rules, or server configuration. Remove unnecessary files.

vulnerabilities.description: Found 11 subdomains: www.netflix.com, mail.netflix.com, blog.netflix.com, api.netflix.com, app.netflix.com, vpn.netflix.com, cdn.netflix.com, static.netflix.com, media.netflix.com, shop.netflix.com, m.netflix.com. Each subdomain increases the attack surface.
vulnerabilities.fix: Audit all subdomains. Ensure unused subdomains are removed and all active ones are secured.
